OP16 Pre-Release Sealed Complete Visual Handbook

Prepared for: Hamandtis aka "Praying Mantis"

Default 2K counter target: 10-12. If you opened 15, start at 11 and adjust by pool strength.

Leader Visual Guide

Portgas.D.Ace - OP16-001

Aggro/pressure leader. Upgrade Whitebeard Pirates and 8000-power packages.

Monkey.D.Luffy - OP16-022

Impel Down tempo. Best when your pool has enough Impel Down bodies.

Buggy - OP16-041

Board flood leader. Good if your pool makes many small bodies.

Sengoku - OP16-060

Navy/admiral ramp. Powerful but wants the right Navy density.

Yamato - OP16-079

Trash/rush plan. Needs support; can punish slow sealed decks.

Marshall.D.Teach - OP16-080

Defensive manipulation. Upgrade trigger cards and Blackbeard Pirates.

Best Cards to Look For - Rated Watchlist

Edward.Newgate - OP16-003 SR

Rating: 10/10
Role: Auto-include bomb
What if: Play if opened. Build around 8000-power support only if your pool has enough; still strong as a finisher.

Monkey.D.Luffy - OP16-015 SR

Rating: 9.5/10
Role: Premium defensive threat
What if: Better with Ace/8000-power package. If unsupported, still consider due defensive pressure and 6000 body.

Boa Hancock - OP16-032 SR

Rating: 9/10
Role: Unblockable finisher
What if: Great when games stall. If curve is already heavy, keep as one of few top-end threats.

Portgas.D.Ace - OP16-118 SEC

Rating: 9/10
Role: Value engine / 2K boost plan
What if: Excellent if you have many 8000-power characters. If not, evaluate as a 5-cost 6000 with search/value upside.

Sakazuki - OP16-065 SR

Rating: 9/10
Role: Huge tempo swing
What if: Best with Navy/Purple support, but -6000 power is relevant even in sealed when it enables clean attacks/removal.

Borsalino - OP16-073 R

Rating: 8.5/10
Role: Threat plus blocker role
What if: Strong if you can afford 7-costs. Do not overload with too many 7+ cards.

Kuzan - OP16-063 R

Rating: 8/10
Role: DON ramp and anti-blocker pressure
What if: Good in slower pools. Downgrade if your deck already has too much top-end or too little early defense.

2K Counter Simulator

ScenarioRunAdjustment
Opened 15 total 2Ks10-12Auto-lock best 9. Add 10th/11th. Add 12th if playable body or you need defense. Cut 13-15 first.
Weak removal11-13You need more hand defense because you cannot answer threats cleanly.
5+ strong removal9-11Removal already protects you, so excessive counters can reduce pressure.
Aggro/tempo build8-10You need attackers. Too many counters slows your clock.
Midrange build10-12Best default: defend early, win with bigger midgame cards.
Control/defensive build11-13Only if your late game is strong enough to reward survival.
Bad 2K bodies/effects8-10Defense-only cards weaken board development.
Good 2K bodies/effects11-13Playable 2Ks are premium because they work in hand and on board.
Rule: Do not play all 15 2K counters blindly. Keep threats, removal, and blockers.
